title: Retire op-twin orchestrator scripts in favor of the reflecting CLI; new ops are op-only
context: The op+script 'dual-surface' (a Pi op AND a hand-written scripts/orchestrator/<op>.ts twin over one library fn) predates pi-context-cli. The reflecting CLI now derives EVERY op as a shell command automatically, so a hand-authored script twin per op is redundant duplication — two shell surfaces to keep in sync instead of one reflected from the ops (the duplication R-0001/cli-core flagged). The op-twin scripts still retain ergonomics the CLI lacks (array_key derivation, --format render, --show-schema, AJV translation, shorthands, exit codes — FGAP-019..026) and historically doubled as a test surface + executable specs (DEC-0019). Surfaced while scoping FGAP-030 (adding a CLI surface for /context check-status): adding a born-obsolete orchestrator-script twin to a brand-new op is pure waste.
decision: (1) NEW ops are op-ONLY — no orchestrator-script twin; the reflecting pi-context-cli IS the shell surface. (2) EXISTING op-twin orchestrator scripts retire once the CLI reaches script-parity (the cli-parity release closing FGAP-019..026); until then they stay because they carry ergonomics the CLI lacks. (3) NON-op scripts — composers (compile-*-context, inject-context-items, file-block-item's multi-step ergonomics), runtime-demos, launch-support (read-config-operations) — are NOT op-twins and REMAIN (or later fold into CLI flows). (4) The scripts-as-test-surface role (DEC-0019) transfers to CLI dogfooding, already the mandated test surface. (5) parity-check's dual-surface op<->script check INVERTS: an op no longer requires a same-named script twin; the CLI is the twin (updated as part of the retire work). (6) FGAP-030 (check-status) is therefore op-only.

options_considered.label: Keep the op+script dual-surface for every op
options_considered.description: Continue shipping a hand-written orchestrator script alongside each op.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Two shell surfaces over one library, kept in sync by parity-check; every new op carries a born-obsolete script.
options_considered.rejected_reason: Rejected — duplication the reflecting CLI eliminates; wastes effort on born-obsolete artifacts.

options_considered.label: Op-only + CLI-as-shell-surface; retire op-twin scripts post-CLI-parity
options_considered.description: New ops op-only; existing op-twin scripts retire after the CLI gains their ergonomics; non-op scripts stay.
options_considered.tradeoffs: Requires the cli-parity release (FGAP-019..026) to land before retiring existing scripts, so capability is not lost.

consequences: No new orchestrator-script twins are authored from now; new ops are op-only (the CLI reflects them).
FGAP-030 (check-status CLI surface) is op-only — drop the orchestrator-script from its resolution.
Amends CLAUDE.md's 'Orchestrator scripts dual-surface' convention ('New substrate op = library + Pi tool + CLI script as a unit') and the feedback_orchestrator_scripts_dual_surface mandate.
Partially supersedes feedback_scripts_as_test_surface / DEC-0019: the test-surface role moves to CLI dogfooding (feedback_use_cli_directly_dogfooding).
Prerequisite for retiring EXISTING op-twin scripts: the cli-parity release (TASK-015..019 closing FGAP-019..026) must land first so the CLI carries the scripts' ergonomics.

TASK-019's behavioral parity gate reframes from op-vs-script-twin to op-vs-CLI (or is folded into the retire work); the actual script deletion + parity-check inversion is a post-cli-parity task, not done now.
Non-op scripts (composers / runtime-demos / launch-support) are explicitly retained.

references.label: the op-only follow-on this decision reshapes
